task_completion.description: Verify the creation of Cortical Bone, Full Bone, and Soft Tissue masks with correct thresholds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Masks' panel (usually bottom-left). You should see multiple masks created. Check the threshold ranges if visible: Cortical Bone (662 to 1988), Full Bone (226 to 3071), and Soft Tissue (-700 to 225).

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the Cancellous Bone mask via Boolean subtraction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Boolean operations dialog being used, specifically subtracting the Cortical Bone mask from the Full Bone mask, or a resulting mask named 'Cancellous Bone' appearing in the Masks panel.

task_completion.description: Verify 3D surfaces are generated, smoothed/decimated, and soft tissue is semi-transparent.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the '3D Surfaces' panel and the 3D viewer. You should see the agent using advanced surface options (smoothing iterations >= 5, decimation). In the 3D viewer, the Soft Tissue (skin/head) surface must be semi-transparent, allowing the skull bone to be visible underneath.

task_completion.description: Verify the placement of at least 3 linear measurements and 1 angular measurement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D slice views or the 3D viewer. You should see at least three linear measurement lines (e.g., front-to-back of skull, side-to-side, and across an orbit) and one angular measurement arc (three points forming an angle, representing the cranial base angle).

task_completion.description: Verify the 3D viewer is oriented for anterior/lateral views and a sagittal clipping plane is used.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 3D viewer. The agent must position the camera to face the front of the skull (anterior), the side of the skull (lateral), and use the clipping plane tool to slice the 3D model sagittally (down the middle) to reveal the internal bone structure.

task_completion.description: Verify the export of STL, PLY, PNG screenshots, and the INV3 project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for file save dialogs. The agent should export 'cortical_bone.stl', 'soft_tissue.ply', take screenshots ('anterior_view.png', 'lateral_view.png', 'cross_section.png'), and save the project as 'case_package.inv3' in the /home/ga/Documents/case_conference/ directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must click through the InVesalius interface to create masks, surfaces, and measurements. It should not just open a terminal and run a script to generate dummy files.

integrity.description: Verify measurements are placed on actual anatomical features.
integrity.visual_evidence: Measurement lines and angles should span across the skull/anatomy in the viewers, not just be randomly clicked in empty black space to artificially inflate the measurement count.

integrity.description: Verify the clipping plane actually intersects the 3D model.
integrity.visual_evidence: When the clipping plane is active for the cross-section screenshot, it must visibly cut through the bone/tissue mesh, showing a hollow or cross-sectional interior, rather than just floating outside the model.

privileged_info_for_vlm: InVesalius 3 thresholds: Cortical Bone = 662 to 1988 HU, Full Bone = 226 to 3071 HU, Soft Tissue = -700 to 225 HU. Cancellous bone is created by Boolean subtraction (Full Bone MINUS Cortical Bone). The Cortical Bone 3D surface must be smoothed (>=5 iterations) and decimated (<300,000 triangles). The Soft Tissue 3D surface must be semi-transparent. Measurements expected: AP diameter (~170-200mm), biparietal width (~130-155mm), orbital dimension (~24-49mm), and cranial base angle (~125-135 degrees). All files must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/case_conference/.
